summary: We are in a pivotal execution phase, balancing the launch of the Intelligent China webpage and Asia Start campaign with the foundational planning of the China Going Global report and a knowledge website redesign.
summaryDetail: Top priority this week is launching the Intelligent China webpage and kicking off the Asia Start campaign with dual AI and traditional routes, as decided on June 23. Simultaneously, the team is advancing the China Going Global report with IE, targeting a September launch at Singapore events; Felix is to deliver a one-pager proposal, and Julie is checking with Tech in Asia for co-launch. The knowledge website redesign is converging after reviewing Joker's and Joseph's proposals, with a decision to show more articles and bring indices forward; Joseph and Julie need to mark feedback and share HTML. Urgent actions include expediting social media agency selection (Media Minds likely), auditing conversion tracking, and finalizing the Shun Tian Shu press release (single release incorporating 10 points). Upcoming: SEO prioritization for Asia Star and Intelligent China over US partnerships, and translating the Chinese report for English review.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's and Joseph's design proposals for the knowledge website homepage, noting that Joker's version mainly changes the menu while the rest is similar to current site. They discussed combining elements from both, such as showing more articles and bringing indices to the front.

meetings.title: SEO and Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: The team discusses improving search visibility for CKGSB programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China, and auditing current conversion tracking setup.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, CES quotes, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency scouting. Decided to streamline Shun Tian Shu press release and expedite agency selection.

meetings.title: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Meeting to plan a joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with a working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'. The report will feature research from Professor Leeway, op-eds from leadership, and industry case studies, aiming for a September launch at events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and discussed a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with modules in Beijing, Shanghai/Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: China-Europe Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on China-Europe relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Videos will not have their own category on the homepage; they will be dispersed by topic.
The homepage should show more articles and bring indices (e.g., BCI, BSI) to the front.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; content will be optimized for search.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or converting to 10-point article.
Expedite social media agency selection; consider Media Minds as likely best option.
The report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with joint distribution.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Report to be ready by early September for launch at Singapore events (Tech in Asia Sept 15-16, Tyson Sept 29-30).
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Report composition: main research from Professor Leeway, op-eds from President Santiago, Dean of CKGSB, and possibly Mapping, plus industry case studies.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will be marketed with a focus on module one (Beijing), targeting foreigners.
Use the editorial design direction for Knowledge revamp.
Use Flow for design of China Going Global report for more polished result and flexibility.
